misconceptions.title: Incorrect Boundary Line Style
misconceptions.description: Students confuse when to use dashed versus solid lines to represent strict versus inclusive inequalities. This error stems from not understanding the visual representation of inequality symbols on a graph.
successIndicators: Use dashed lines for strict inequalities
Use solid lines for inclusive inequalities

misconceptions.title: Sign Errors in Equation Manipulation
misconceptions.description: Mistakes arise when students switch signs improperly, affecting the equation outcome. This error occurs due to misunderstanding when sign changes are necessary, like during multiplication or division of inequalities.

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Shading Region for Inequalities
misconceptions.description: Shading errors result from misidentification of solution regions on a graph. Students fail to establish which part of the graph satisfies the inequality conditions.

misconceptions.title: Misapplication of Inverse Operations
misconceptions.description: Incorrect use of inverse operations, like adding instead of subtracting, indicates a misunderstanding of how to maintain equation balance.
